title: Why You Need To Replace Those Windows 98 And NT Machines
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Aberdeen Group analyst argues that 50M+ aging PCs running Windows 98 and NT in corporate environments pose escalating security and compliance risks as Microsoft ends support. Recommends immediate PC replacement cycle citing Windows XP Pro SP1 stability; Windows Server 2003 platform; Intel Springdale chipset and Centrino mobile platform; auditor scrutiny of unsupported systems; and potential legal liability for negligence. Published at critical juncture of Windows lifecycle transition.
importance_rationale: Timely call-to-action during a critical Windows lifecycle transition; articulated security and compliance risks that many enterprises were ignoring; written by a respected analyst at a critical juncture for enterprise PC refresh.
relevance_rationale: Specific OS and hardware recommendations entirely obsolete; however the lifecycle management argument and security-compliance framework remain highly relevant templates for current end-of-life technology transitions such as Windows 10 EOL 2025.
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted that security vulnerabilities in unsupported OS would become critical business risk; the auditor and legal liability arguments proved prescient as regulatory compliance (SOX HIPAA PCI-DSS) increasingly mandated supported software; Intel platform predictions (Springdale and Centrino dominance) were accurate; the shift to laptops (one-third of purchases) marked the beginning of mobile computing dominance.
